---
title: "Analyzing US Births and Basketball Recruits"
author: "Ishika Ravat"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
births_tibble <- read_xlsx("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx") |>
  mutate(day_of_week = factor(day_of_week,
                              levels = c("Sun", "Mon", "Tues", "Wed", "Thurs", "Fri", "Sat"),
                              ordered = TRUE))
```

```{r}
births_tibble |>
  group_by(month, date_of_month) |>
  summarize(mean_births = mean(births, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = date_of_month, y = month, fill = mean_births)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_y_reverse(breaks = 1:12, labels = month.abb) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 31, by = 5)) +
  labs(x = "Day of Month", y = "Month", fill = "Average Births") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
christmas_data <- births_tibble |>
  filter(month == 12) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    christmas = births[date_of_month == 25],
    baseline = mean(births[date_of_month %in% c(20:24, 27:30)], na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_of_baseline = (christmas / baseline) * 100,
    day_of_week = day_of_week[date_of_month == 25]
  )

summary(christmas_data)
```

```{r}
ggplot(christmas_data, aes(x = year, y = pct_of_baseline)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(aes(color = day_of_week)) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Christmas Births as % of Baseline",
    color = "Day of Week",
    title = "US Christmas Births Relative to Baseline (1994-2014)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
births_model <- lm(births ~ factor(year) + factor(month) + day_of_week, data = births_tibble)

summary(births_model)$r.squared
```

```{r}
births_adjusted <- births_tibble |>
  mutate(pct_resid = residuals(births_model) / mean(births, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
```

```{r}
calendar_resid <- births_adjusted |>
  filter(!(month == 2 & date_of_month == 29)) |>
  group_by(month, date_of_month) |>
  summarize(mean_pct_resid = mean(pct_resid, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(calendar_date = make_date(year = 2001, month = month, day = date_of_month))
```

```{r}
holidays <- tibble(
  calendar_date = as.Date(c("2001-01-01", "2001-07-04", "2001-12-25")),
  mean_pct_resid = c(-24.3, -26.1, -39.4),
  label = c("New Year's Day", "Independence Day", "Christmas Day"),
  hjust = c(-0.1, 0.5, 1.1),
  vjust = c(0.5, 1.5, 0.5)
)

ggplot(calendar_resid, aes(x = calendar_date, y = mean_pct_resid)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_point(data = holidays, color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_text(data = holidays, aes(label = label, hjust = hjust, vjust = vjust), color = "blue") +
  labs(
    x = "Calendar Date",
    y = "Mean Residuals (% of Mean Births)",
    title = "Average Daily Residuals of US Births (1994-2014)"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
basketball_tibble <- read_excel("data/nba_recruits.xlsx") |>
  mutate(tier = factor(tier,
                       levels = c("Never played", "Brief career", "Solid career", "All-Star level", "Superstar"),
                       ordered = TRUE)) |>
  mutate(recruit_group = factor(recruit_group,
                                levels = c("#1–10", "#11–25", "#26–50", "#51–100", "Outside top 100"),
                                ordered = TRUE))

basketball_tibble
```

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
notable_players <- basketball_tibble |>
  filter(name %in% c("LeBron James", "Kevin Durant", "Draymond Green", "Kawhi Leonard"))

basketball_tibble |>
  filter(!is.na(rank), !is.na(top_mean_wa)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = rank, y = top_mean_wa)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = tier), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_text(data = notable_players, aes(label = name), vjust = -0.8, hjust = 0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "Recruit Rank",
    y = "Wins Above Baseline",
    color = "Career Tier",
    title = "NBA Recruit Performance: Rank vs. Wins Above Baseline"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
